entry.title: BIP 118 and SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
entry.summary:
The correspondence sheds light on a challenging aspect of peer-to-peer (P2P) protocol design, particularly concerning the relaying of transactions in blockchain networks. The primary issue highlighted is the need for a protocol that allows transactions to be relayed to a full node without the necessity of knowing which input transactions the receiving node has in its mempool or active chain. This capability is especially critical for systems like Lightning Network, where it is often unclear which commitment transactions from counterparties are present in a node’s mempool.
The problem is described as highly complex due to implications for both P2P protocol intricacies and mempool management. The discussion suggests that without addressing these complexities, certain proposed solutions such as SIGHASH_NOINPUT may not be effective for the intended purposes within the Lightning framework. To potentially mitigate these challenges, the idea of integrating an external overlay relay network is proposed. This network could facilitate proper transaction relay, potentially involving miners running specific software to support this function.
This conversation emphasizes the intricate relationship between transaction relay protocols and overall network efficiency in blockchain technologies. It also highlights the ongoing need for innovative consensus designs and external support systems to enhance transaction processing in decentralized networks.
